reasoning: The article describes the use of citrate-capped gold nanoparticles (AuNPs) as peroxidase mimetics for the detection of Cr(VI) through a colorimetric assay. The AuNPs catalyze the oxidation of TMB by Cr(VI), resulting in a blue color change. The study investigates the kinetic parameters, selectivity, and detection performance of the assay. The AuNPs used have a size of approximately 15 nm and are highly negatively charged, which enhances their catalytic activity due to electrostatic interactions with TMB. The assay is performed at room temperature and requires about half an hour, including the preparation of AuNPs. The detection limit for Cr(VI) is estimated to be 0.52 μg/L, and the assay shows high selectivity against other interfering ions. The method is applied to the determination of Cr(VI) in spiked water samples, with recoveries ranging from 95.00% to 105.40%.
